body_txt: An easy way to tell if a female character in Japanese media is of high social standing is to look at her head. No, not the top of her head for any tiara or other Requisite Royal Regalia , but her hair — specifically, the locks which directly frame her face. If you see a curl directly in front of or directly behind each ear — always in front of her shoulder, never tucked behind — she either is high class or acts like it. Needless to say, this is common among Ojou . Some Elegant Gothic Lolitas also sport this hairstyle. Also known as "drill hair", and derived from the Regal Ringlets popular in Roman times. For the distinctly Japanese princess' hairstyle — long and straight with level bangs and sidelocks — see Hime Cut . Please do not list examples unless they have two curls from either the hair directly in front of or directly behind the ear and at least one of the lifestyle or personality characteristics (royalty, Ojou , Elegant Gothic Lolita , Lady of War , or other traits). Having two ringlets only does not qualify a hairstyle as Ojou Ringlets . Do not confuse them for the sideburns Orthodox Jewish men wear, called peyot (pronounced "pay-ott") ( peyos , pronounced "pay-ohs," if you're Ashkenazi ). A Sub-Trope of Regal Ringlets . Compare Quirky Curls .
